mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) is a direct measure of expanding supply-side activity in the economy. Consistent positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO growth against forward 60-day returns fails to exceed 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal is not significantly positive during periods of rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 9.6% signals a slack labor market. A drop in UNRATE below 9.0 indicates improving employment conditions, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 9.0 but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative more often than positive, or the average return is not significantly greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 0.19%) signals an accommodative monetary environment that stimulates housing demand. This joint condition predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days as the housing recovery strengthens.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with nascent housing recovery
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional return is not significantly above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 8%), a decrease in UNRATE of at least 0.2 percentage points month-over-month signals a material improvement in the labor market, which reduces recession fears and supports equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If under the regime condition (UNRATE > 8%) and the trigger condition, forward 20-day returns are negative on average or the strategy's Sharpe ratio over the walk-forward period is below 0.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) increasing month-over-month is a direct indicator of consumer demand strength. Positive growth in RSAFS drives immediate positive sentiment and stock returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants react to strong consumption data.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 5-day returns fails to be positive and statistically significant (p-value > 0.10) over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP (GDPC1) grows quarter-over-quarter but industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month, it signals a structural weakness in the manufacturing sector despite headline growth. This divergence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as the imbalance corrects.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 0 and INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0
failure_condition: If under the trigger condition forward 60-day returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in inflation, measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) month-over-month change exceeding 0.4%, signals overheating and potential monetary tightening. This inflation scare negatively impacts equity valuations over the next 20 trading days due to discount rate increases and reduced real earnings.
failure_condition: If triggers are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in both payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) provides a comprehensive labor market signal. When PAYEMS rises month-over-month and UNRATE falls month-over-month, it indicates robust job creation and declining slack, which together predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS > previous month and UNRATE < previous month
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields forward 60-day returns that are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the strategy fails to outperform a buy-and-hold over the walk-forward period.
